summary: A Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts` because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned a URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expected the URL without a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This caused the entire test suite to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` in `test/config/get.ts` at line 32 fails because the actual output of `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` includes a trailing slash in the registry URL, but the test expects the URL without a trailing slash.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific failing test file and test name.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: the received value has a trailing slash that the expected value does not.
Pinpoints the exact file and line number where the assertion failure occurred.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly 1 test suite and 1 test failed, consistent with the single assertion failure identified.
Shows the test runner exited with status 1 due to the failing test, propagating the failure to the CI job.
Confirms the CI job failed with exit code 1.

suggested_fix: Update the test assertion in `test/config/get.ts` at line 32 to accept a trailing slash (change expected value to `'https://my-org.registry.example.com/'`), or fix the `pnpm config get` implementation to strip trailing slashes from registry URLs before outputting them.
